page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The primary hardware accelerator is a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The CPU exhibits weak single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, placing in the bottom 25%. However, the system achieves top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. For small 135M and medium 7B models, prompt processing and text generation speeds rank in the top 10%, though no data is available for larger 70B models. Memory bandwidth and standard database operations perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-8 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades raw CPU compute power for high-density GPU acceleration. It is highly optimized for workloads that offload processing to the GPU, such as low-to-medium scale LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, and graphics rendering. It is less suitable for CPU-heavy compilation, intensive text processing, or storage-dependent database hosting due to its weak CPU benchmarks and lack of local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine configured with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor providing 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks like Geekbench and stress-ng place this instance in the bottom 25% for both single-core and multi-core performance, its GPU-accelerated capabilities deliver top-tier results for large language model inference. Specifically, prompt processing and text generation for 135M and 7B models rank in the top 10% of tested systems. This server does not include local storage. It represents a cost-efficient option for GPU-centric workloads, such as machine learning inference and graphics rendering, where high-performance CPU compute is not the primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference but weaker CPU performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 features 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, offering top-tier LLM inference with weak CPU performance.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine optimized for low-to-medium scale large language model inference and graphics rendering workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Features 8 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture with 32 GB RAM
Delivers top-tier performance for 135M and 7B LLM inference
Exhibits weak single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Does not include local SSD storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for GPU-bound workloads, machine learning inference, and rendering
